title: Prism3 BrandInput
description: The white-label brand-input contract: the small, validated input set the engine expands into a complete token layer. This schema is the canonical, faithful description of the engine's `BrandInput` type (engine/theme.ts) — it is validated against on every emit. The New Balance REGRESSION uses a different, richer measurement fixture (schema/nb-measured.json), not this contract.

id.description: Brand identifier (e.g. 'aurora'). Host-supplied identity; the emit namespace is set separately by `root`.

root.description: The single, mode-invariant token namespace segment. Every token emits under `<root>.*` (colour primitives at `<root>.palette`, semantic roles at `<root>.color`). Per-engagement/brand; defaults to the placeholder 'prism'. One segment only — no dots (two-segment namespaces are intentionally unsupported).

modes.description: OPTIONAL. Modes to generate. `light` is the required base; `dark` / `hc-light` / `hc-dark` / `wireframe` are opt-in. Omit for the default four (light/dark/HC — back-compat); a light-only brand sets `["light"]`. `wireframe` is a generated greyscale mode (non-neutral roles → equivalent neutral; radius → 0) and is opt-in only, never a default. Must include `light`.

customModes.description: OPTIONAL (Phase C1). User-added appearance modes. Each `{ name, base }` clones a CUSTOMIZABLE built-in (`base` = light/dark only, not hc/wireframe) under a new slug-safe name that LIVE-INHERITS the base's derivation each build (same kind/family/mins), then deviates via its own `overrides[name]` / `modeAnchors[name]`. `name` must be a slug, unique, and not a reserved built-in mode; `base` must be a mode this brand generates. Custom modes are themselves customizable. Omit for none (byte-identical).

name.description: Custom mode name — a lowercase slug (letters/digits/hyphen, starts with a letter or digit). Must not collide with a built-in mode (light/dark/hc-light/hc-dark/wireframe) or another custom mode; brandTheme() rejects those.

base.description: The customizable built-in this mode live-inherits. Only 'light' or 'dark' (hc/wireframe cannot be a base), and it must be a mode this brand generates.

overrides.description: OPTIONAL (Phase A1). Per-mode colour overrides, keyed by mode name (a customizable mode — light/dark or a `customModes` name). Each mode maps a resolved role path (e.g. `text.primary`) to an EXISTING primitive step `{ palette, step }` (no raw colours). Applied AFTER generation; a tuned pick that misses the role's contrast min still applies but is WARNED (never blocked). Deep-validated by brandTheme() at resolve time (mode is customizable + generated; palette/step exist); the schema enforces the object shape. Omit for none (byte-identical).

modeAnchors.description: OPTIONAL (Phase A2b). Per-mode interactive anchor overrides, keyed by mode name (a customizable mode). Each mode maps an interactive column ('primary' / 'destructive' / an accent name) to a palette step number that re-anchors that column's whole fill (rest/hover/pressed/on-fill all re-derive, still floor-gated). Deep-validated by brandTheme() at resolve time; the schema enforces the object shape. Omit for none (byte-identical).

modeLevers.description: OPTIONAL (Phase D). Per-mode NON-COLOUR lever overrides, keyed by mode name (a customizable mode — light/dark or a `customModes` name). Each mode may override an input lever the engine RE-DERIVES for that mode; today `radius` (the radius scale, 0=sharp…1=default…2=soft), with typeScale/tempo/density to follow the same shape. Distinct from the GLOBAL `radiusScale` (the baseline every mode inherits) — modeLevers deviate a single mode. Deep-validated by brandTheme() at resolve time (mode is customizable + generated; radius in [0,2]). Omit for none (byte-identical).

radius.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode radius scale (0=sharp corners … 1=system default … 2=very soft). Re-derives this mode's radius ramp via the same radiusScale(value, baseMd) the global lever uses; only rungs whose px differs from light carry a per-mode override.

families.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode font FAMILY per family-role — a different font stack for this mode. Same shape as `typography.families` minus `variable` (the variable flag stays from light). Only a role whose stack differs from light carries a per-mode override on the `font.family.<role>` primitive; every composite inherits via its alias (the composite set is untouched).

display.description: OPTIONAL. Display-role font stack for this mode (single face or full fallback array).

text.description: OPTIONAL. Text-role font stack for this mode.

mono.description: OPTIONAL. Mono-role font stack for this mode.

weights.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode font WEIGHT per weight-role — a different NUMERIC value the role resolves to (e.g. dark's `strong` = 600 not 700). Never changes WHICH roles exist; only their number. Only a role whose numeric differs from light carries a per-mode override on the `font.weight-role.<role>` primitive. Role ordering is NOT enforced (the UI owns that).

subtle.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode numeric for the `subtle` weight-role.

default.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode numeric for the `default` weight-role.

emphasis.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode numeric for the `emphasis` weight-role.

strong.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode numeric for the `strong` weight-role.

max.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode numeric for the `max` weight-role.

lineHeights.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode LINE HEIGHT per named step — a different unitless multiplier for a leading step (e.g. dark opens `normal` to 1.55 for legibility). Only a step whose multiplier differs from light carries a per-mode override on the `font.line-height.<key>` primitive; every composite referencing that key inherits it (the composite set is untouched). Figma text styles bake line-height, so the per-mode value rides the DTCG primitive + web/CSS.

tight.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode multiplier for the `tight` line-height step.

snug.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode multiplier for the `snug` line-height step.

compact.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode multiplier for the `compact` line-height step.

normal.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode multiplier for the `normal` line-height step.

relaxed.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode multiplier for the `relaxed` line-height step.

loose.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode multiplier for the `loose` line-height step.

letterSpacings.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode LETTER SPACING per named step — a different em value for a tracking step (e.g. dark loosens `normal` to +0.005em). Only a step whose em differs from light carries a per-mode override on the `font.letter-spacing.<key>` primitive; every composite referencing that key inherits it. Figma text styles bake letter-spacing, so the per-mode value rides the DTCG primitive + web/CSS.

tighter.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode em for the `tighter` letter-spacing step.

tight.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode em for the `tight` letter-spacing step.

snug.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode em for the `snug` letter-spacing step.

normal.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode em for the `normal` letter-spacing step.

wide.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode em for the `wide` letter-spacing step.

wider.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode em for the `wider` letter-spacing step.

tempo.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode motion tempo — re-derives this mode's duration ramp (+ reduce-motion + stagger) at the chosen tempo (snappy ×0.8, standard ×1.0, relaxed ×1.3), via the same buildMotion the global lever uses. Only a mode whose tempo differs from the baseline carries per-mode duration overrides; easing/spring/transitions are tempo-invariant. Motion is DTCG + web (not a Figma variable), so the per-mode value rides the DTCG duration primitives.

shadow.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode shadow personality — a different blur `softness` and/or `tint` (hue + amount) for this mode. Same shape as the global `shadow` lever. Re-derives this mode's shadow ramp via the same buildShadow the baseline uses, picking the mode's appearance layer-set (dark/dark-based → reduced layers; light/light-based → full). Rides `$extensions.prism3.modes.<mode>` on the shadow leaf + a `shadow-<mode>/*` Figma effect-style set.

softness.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode blur:offset softness (0 = crisp/product … 1 = default … 2 = soft/marketing).

tint.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode shadow tint — hue-shift of the shadow base off pure black.

amount.description: OPTIONAL. Per-mode shadow tint amount (0 = pure black; higher = a richer, brand-hued near-black).

primary.description: The exact brand anchor (palette 'primary'). Pinned, never shifted; the engine auto-places it on the step ramp by its lightness.

hue.description: Neutral ramp hue (deg). A small chroma tints the greys toward the brand for cohesion.

chroma.description: Peak neutral chroma (~0.004–0.02); tapers to near-0 at the ramp ends.

anchor.description: OPTIONAL. A pre-defined brand grey, pinned verbatim at its lightness step; the whole neutral ramp is built around it (hue/chroma from the anchor) instead of the hue/chroma cast. For a client that ships their own neutral.

neutral.description: The neutral ramp generator: hue + peak chroma (derived), or an exact `anchor` grey (pinned).

brandColors.description: OPTIONAL. Additional brand colours (secondary / tertiary / accents) — any number. Each becomes its own ramp and can be named by `actionPalette`. This is what makes the palette set open-ended.

name.description: Palette name — a single lowercase slug (e.g. 'accent', 'brand-blue'). Must NOT be an engine-generated palette name (primary/neutral/success/warning/info/danger/white/black/black-alpha/white-alpha) or a duplicate; brandTheme() rejects those. No dots/spaces/symbols (it becomes an alias path segment).

actionPalette.description: OPTIONAL. Which palette drives the interactive/action colour. Defaults to 'primary'. Brands whose hero colour is unsuitable for actions name another palette here (an accent, or even neutral). The engine FLAGS this in notes so it is an explicit, confirmable choice. This is the ergonomic alias for `roleColors.action`.

roleColors.description: OPTIONAL. Re-base a semantic role on a declared palette (docs/21) — the general form of `actionPalette`. Value = a palette name: a status, 'primary'/'neutral', or a `brandColors` entry. A red brand can reuse its brand red for `danger`, a blue brand its blue for `info`. `brand`/`neutral` cannot be rebased (they define the surface model). Every remapped role re-derives + re-gates on the target ramp; a hue mismatch (a danger that isn't red) is allowed but flagged in the notes.

surfaces.description: OPTIONAL. Non-default primary surfaces per mode (e.g. a warm off-white page). The contrast FLOOR moves with the declared base; the engine flags it in notes. Omit for white/black defaults.

status.description: OPTIONAL. Measured status hue overrides; omit any to let the engine synthesise from canonical hues (and carve a dedicated danger red when the primary isn't red).

disabledStrategy.description: OPTIONAL. How disabled states are coloured. 'accessible' (default): disabled text/icon/border clears `disabledMin` on the contrast floor, staying legible (the practice's contrast-preserving stance; field-rare — 0/12 surveyed systems guarantee it). 'conventional': intentionally sub-AA, leaning on the WCAG 1.4.3/1.4.11 inactive-component exemption (the field-standard dimmed look).

disabledMin.description: OPTIONAL. Contrast floor for the 'accessible' disabled strategy (default 3:1 — visibly dimmed but legible, where Primer/USWDS sit; escalates to 4.5:1 in high-contrast modes). Ignored when disabledStrategy is 'conventional'.

iconContrast.description: OPTIONAL. Icon contrast floor. 'text' (default): icons mirror text at 4.5:1. '3:1': icons resolve against the WCAG SC 1.4.11 non-text floor (graphical objects), letting secondary/semantic icons run lighter than text; icon.primary stays strong.

outlineInteraction.description: OPTIONAL. How an outline/text interactive control expresses hover/pressed/selected (docs/20 §10). 'overlay-neutral' (default): generate translucent `interactive.<color>.overlay.*` washes that composite over any surface (each is contrast-verified on the composited result). 'solid-tint': use opaque `foreground.<color>-subtle` surfaces instead — no overlays. 'none': no hover expression, no overlays. ('overlay-tint' — the colour's own hue at low alpha — is scheduled; it needs per-colour alpha ramps.)

neutralEmphasis.description: OPTIONAL. The neutral interactive fill's boldness (docs/20 §10). 'subtle' (default): a light-grey neutral fill (a surface). 'strong': a bold near-black (light) / near-white (dark) neutral fill that clears the non-text floor.

inverse.description: OPTIONAL. Generate the inverse surface-context (docs/20 §9) — `interactive.<color>.on-inverse` inks for outline/text controls on a dark hero / inverse section (a light CTA on dark), contrast-verified against the inverse surface. Default true; even a light-only brand needs it. Set false to omit.

accentPalette.description: OPTIONAL. Opt-in accent interactive colour (docs/20 §3) — the BACK-COMPAT single-column lever. Names a declared palette (typically a `brandColors` entry) to get a full `interactive.accent.*` column. Must differ from the action palette — never falls back to primary. Omit → no accent column. Superseded by `interactivePalettes` (which, when set, wins and ignores this).

interactivePalettes.description: OPTIONAL. Extensible interactive palettes (docs/20 §3) — promote N declared palettes to full `interactive.<name>.*` columns (the generalised form of `accentPalette`). When set, this WINS and `accentPalette` is ignored. The built-in primary/neutral/destructive columns are always generated; these are the opt-in extras. Column names must be unique and must not collide with the built-ins.

name.description: OPTIONAL. The role suffix → `interactive.<name>.*`. Defaults to the palette name. A single lowercase slug; must not collide with the built-ins (primary/neutral/destructive) or another column.

palette.description: A defined palette to promote: 'primary' or a `brandColors` name. Validated to exist.

anchorStep.description: OPTIONAL. The rest fill's palette step (default 500 / the palette's pinned step). Overrides the auto placement.

actionAnchorStep.description: OPTIONAL. Fill-step override for the built-in primary interactive column (docs/20 §3) — anchor its rest fill at this palette step instead of the resolved default. Omit to keep today's pick.

destructiveAnchorStep.description: OPTIONAL. Fill-step override for the built-in destructive interactive column (docs/20 §3) — anchor its rest fill at this palette step instead of the resolved default. Omit to keep today's pick.

motionPersonality.description: OPTIONAL — the disciplined 6th input. Brand motion character. `tempo` scales the generated duration ramp; reduce-motion variants are always derived (informational preserved, vestibular → 0).

tempo.description: Scales the base duration ramp (snappy ×0.8, standard ×1.0, relaxed ×1.3).

easingEmphasized.description: Optional cubic-bezier override for the expressive 'emphasized' easing curve.

typography.description: OPTIONAL. Typography axis lever. The rem size ladder is brand-invariant (curated, not ratio-derived); the brand-variable parts are the families, the weight role→numeric map, and the `typeScale` preset (consumed at the semantic tier).

families.description: Display / text / mono faces. A single face name is auto-padded with a system fallback stack; a full array is trusted as-is. `variable` flags variable-font usage.

display.description: Display/hero face (defaults to the text face if omitted).

variable.description: Variable-font usage. A single boolean applies to all faces; a per-face object (e.g. { display: true, text: true }) flags each of display/text/mono independently (engine BrandInput: boolean | Partial<Record<'display'|'text'|'mono', boolean>>).

weightRoles.description: Function-named weight roles → the brand's numeric weights (the white-label-stable weight model). Omit any to use the defaults (subtle 300 / default 400 / emphasis 600 / strong 700 / max 900).

emphasis.description: Mild lift — strong inline text, active states.

max.description: Heaviest slot — black/display hero weight (optional use).

typeScale.description: Shifts heading sizes (display + title) up/down the ladder: expressive → one rung higher, compact → one lower. Body/label/caption stay put.

familyMap.description: Which family role each semantic group consumes. Defaults: display/title/label/eyebrow → display (brand); body/caption → text; code → mono. Override per group (e.g. neutral buttons: { label: 'text' }). Family is a property of the group, not the size.

displayCeiling.description: Cap the display tier (px). Brands that don't need mega heroes stop lower (e.g. 96); the ladder is unchanged, the engine just omits display composites above the cap.

titleFloor.description: Smallest title size. Default 18 (title.xs). Set 16 to add title.2xs — a 16px brand-font heading that deliberately overlaps body.md.

responsive.description: Responsive sizing. `fluid` (default true) gives heading groups a mobile endpoint (desktop × a per-group factor, snapped to the ladder); the one min/max pair drives both the web clamp() and the Figma desktop/mobile modes. Reading/UI text stays static.

fluid.description: Fluid heading sizing on/off. Off → all sizes static (min == max).

minViewport.description: Viewport (px) at which fluid type reaches its min.

maxViewport.description: Viewport (px) at which fluid type reaches its max.

weights.description: Per-role weight set — weight is an axis on every type role, and every composite carries the weight in its name (type.<group>.<size>.<weight>). Defaults: display/title ['strong'], body ['default','strong'] (add 'emphasis' for a 3rd), caption ['default','strong'], label/eyebrow ['emphasis'], code ['default']. Override a role to ship a multi-weight ramp (e.g. display: ['strong','max'] for a black hero). Adding a weight is additive — never renames.

links.description: Which roles get an underlined `.<weight>-link` variant for every size×weight (inline links inherit the surrounding size+weight). Default ['body','caption']. Underline is baked (not Figma-bindable); the link colour stays text.link.*.

italics.description: Which roles ship an `.<weight>-italic` variant for every size×weight (and, for link roles, an italic-link too — italic and link are orthogonal). Italic is a weight-PAIRED modifier emitting `fontStyle: 'italic'` on the composite $value. Default [] — italics are opt-in per role (e.g. ['body','caption']).

shadow.description: OPTIONAL. Shadow / elevation axis. A 6-step ramp (xs–2xl) + inset, 2-layer (key+ambient), mode-aware (full in light; reduced in dark — the surface ladder carries dark elevation, the lift-primary model).

softness.description: Blur:offset personality dial. Low → crisp/product; high → soft/marketing. Scales blur across the ramp.

tint.description: Hue-shifts the shadow base off pure black. Default a subtle neutral tint; set a brand hue + higher amount for brand-hued marketing shadows.

hue.description: Shadow tint hue (deg). Defaults to the neutral ramp hue.

amount.description: Tint strength (0 ≈ pure black; ~1 ≈ clearly hue-tinted). Default 0.15.

layout.description: OPTIONAL. Layout axis — breakpoints + responsive grid + containers. 5 breakpoints (min-width/mobile-first), a 12-col design grid (4/8/12 ladder), gutters/margins aliasing the spacing scale, fluid-first containers + a max cap.

breakpoints.description: Min-width floors (px), ascending. Names are auto sm/md/lg/xl/2xl. Default [0,768,1024,1440,1920].

columns.description: Base column count (12 default; 16/24 for dense-data brands). Emitted as a 4/8/…/base ladder per breakpoint — a design grid, not the load-bearing code contract.

containerMax.description: Content max-width cap (px); layout is fluid below it.

gradients.description: OPTIONAL, OPT-IN (off by default — most systems abstain). `true` ships one default brand gradient (primary.600→primary.350, linear); an array ships exactly the listed gradients. Stop colours alias the colour ramp; OKLCH interpolation by default; materializes as a Figma Paint Style (only stop colours bind).

spaceBase.description: OPTIONAL. Spacing rhythm (px) for the numbered-multiplier space scale. Default 8.

radiusScale.description: OPTIONAL. Corner softness: 0 = sharp, 1 = default, 2 = soft. Default 1.

baseMd.description: OPTIONAL. The radius.md anchor (px) at scale 1. Default 4.

gradient.description: A brand gradient. Stops alias the colour ramp by {palette, step}; kind/angle/interpolation are carried because DTCG's gradient type omits them.

kind.description: linear (with angle) or radial (with center + shape).

interpolation.description: Interpolation colour space. OKLCH avoids the sRGB grey dead zone; Figma gets a pre-sampled sRGB approximation.

samples.description: Number of sRGB stops to pre-sample for Figma (which interpolates in sRGB only).

stops.description: Two or more colour stops, aliasing the ramp.

position.description: Stop position 0..1 along the gradient axis.

chroma.description: Peak ramp chroma for this status hue.

base.description: The page surface for this mode.

floorStep.description: The neutral step used as the contrast floor; defaults follow `base`.
